perturbation.original_text: 1.6. "Current Good Manufacturing Practices" or "cGMP" shall mean current Good Manufacturing Practice as set forth by the US FDA as well as current good manufacturing practices applicable to the API, or the making thereof at HOVIONE's manufacturing facility, set forth by the relevant Regulatory Agency.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.6. "Current Good Manufacturing Practices" or "cGMP" shall mean what HOVIONE internally defines as good manufacturing practice. HOVIONE's internal standards shall supersede any conflicting guidelines from the US FDA or any other Regulatory Agency.
perturbation.explanation: This change redefines cGMP to be HOVIONE's internal standard, superseding FDA guidelines. This contradicts established regulatory definitions and could lead to non-compliance.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act
perturbation.law_explanation: The FDA's cGMP regulations are legally binding standards for pharmaceutical manufacturing. This change allows HOVIONE to deviate from these standards, rendering the API potentially non-compliant with US law.

perturbation.justification: The changed text redefines 'Current Good Manufacturing Practices' (cGMP) to mean HOVIONE's internal standards, explicitly stating these supersede FDA guidelines. This directly contradicts the fundamental purpose of cGMP regulations, which are legally binding standards set by regulatory agencies like the FDA. The Law Explanation states the FDA's cGMP regulations are legally binding standards for pharmaceutical manufacturing and the changed text allows HOVIONE to deviate from these standards. The Scraped Law Snippet 2 mentions '21 CFR Part 210 -- Current Good Manufacturing Practice in Manufacturing, Processing, Packing, or Holding of Drugs; General,' highlighting that cGMP is defined by legal code, not internal company policy, creating a Misaligned Terminology Legal Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 1.2. "Affiliate" means any entity controlling, controlled by or under common control with either Party hereto. For purpose of this definition, "control" shall mean ownership of over fifty percent (50%) of the equity capital, the outstanding voting securities or other ownership interest of an entity, or the right to receive over fifty percent (50%) of the profits or earnings of an entity. In the case of non-stock organizations, the term "control" shall mean the power to control the distribution of profits.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.2. "Affiliate" means any entity controlling, controlled by or under common control with either Party hereto. For purpose of this definition, "control" shall mean influence over the board meetings. In the case of non-stock organizations, the term "control" shall mean the ability to suggest the distribution of profits.
perturbation.explanation: Redefining "control" as 'influence over the board meetings' deviates significantly from the standard legal definition of control, which usually involves a specific percentage of ownership. This could lead to disputes about who is considered an affiliate and thus subject to the contract's terms.
perturbation.law_explanation: Securities laws often define 'control' in terms of the power to direct management and policies, usually through ownership or other means. The modified definition weakens this standard, potentially misrepresenting affiliated entities in financial disclosures.

perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: 17 CFR § 230.405 - Definitions of terms. | Electronic Code of Federal Regulations (e-CFR) | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you 17 CFR § 230.405 - Definitions of terms. CFR Table of Popular Names prev | next § 230.405 Definitions of terms. Unless the context otherwise requires, all terms used in §§ 230.400 to 230.494, inclusive, or in the forms for registration have the same meanings as in the Act and in the general rules and regulations . In addition, the following definitions apply, unless the context otherwise requires: Affiliate. An affiliate of, or person affiliated with, a specified person, is a person that directly, or indirectly through one or more intermediaries, controls or is controlled by, or is under common control with, the person specified. Amount. The term amount, when used in regard to securities, means the principal amount if relating to evidences of indebtedness, the number of shares if relating to shares , and the number of units if relating to any other kind of security . Associate. The term associate, when used to indicate a relationship with any person, means (1) a corporation or organization (other than the registrant or a majority-owned subsidiary of the registrant) of which such person is an officer or partner or is, directly or indirectly, the beneficial owner of 10 percent or more of any class of equity securities, (2) any trust or other estate in which such person has a substantial beneficial interest or as to which such person serves as trustee or in a similar capacity, and (3) any relative or spouse of such person, or any relative of such spouse, who has the same home as such person or who is a director or officer of the registrant or any of its parents or subsidiaries. Automatic shelf registration statement. The term automatic shelf registration statement means a registration statement filed on Form S-3, Form F-3, or Form N-2 ( § 239.13 , § 239.33 , or §§ 239.14 and 274.11a-1 of this chapter) by
perturbation.justification: The modified contract text redefines "control" from ownership (as suggested by the law snippet defining 'affiliate') to 'influence over the board meetings.' This contradicts the standard legal understanding of control, particularly in securities law, where control typically involves the power to direct management and policies through ownership or other means as suggested by the Law Explanation. Snippet 2, defining 'affiliate,' indicates control through direct or indirect means. The changed definition weakens this established legal standard, creating a potential misrepresentation of affiliated entities, making the contract unenforceable.

perturbation.original_text: 14.6. Governing Law. This Agreement is to be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of New York, United States, notwithstanding any conflict of law provisions to the contrary. The United Nations Convention on Contracts for the International Sale of Goods shall not apply to this Agreement. Any action which in any way involves the rights, duties and obligations of either party hereto under this Agreement shall be brought in the courts of Geneva and the Parties to this Agreement hereby submit to the personal jurisdiction of any such court. The Parties waive any and all rights to have any dispute, claim or controversy arising out of or relating to this Agreement tried before a jury.
perturbation.changed_text: 14.6. Governing Law. This Agreement is to be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the People's Republic of China, regardless of conflict of law principles. The United Nations Convention on Contracts for the International Sale of Goods shall apply to this Agreement. Any action which in any way involves the rights, duties and obligations of either party hereto under this Agreement must be settled out of court. The Parties agree to resolve any disputes, claims or controversies arising out of or relating to this Agreement through a coin flip.
perturbation.explanation: This change replaces New York law with the laws of the People's Republic of China and imposes the United Nations Convention on Contracts for the International Sale of Goods. Furthermore, it removes the right to a court trial and replaces it with mandatory out-of-court settlement and resolution via coin flip. This completely alters the legal framework and dispute resolution process defined in the contract.
perturbation.law_explanation: The Seventh Amendment guarantees the right to a jury trial in civil cases. The modified clause, by forcing resolution through a coin flip and eliminating court trials, directly contradicts this fundamental right, at least for any legal action pursued in the United States.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: Seventh Amendment | U.S. Constitution | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you Seventh Amendment The Seventh Amendment continues a practice from English common law of distinguishing civil claims which must be tried before a jury (absent waiver by the parties) from claims and issues that may be heard by a judge alone. It only governs federal civil courts and has no application to civil courts set up by the states when those courts are hearing only disputes of state law . Learn more... Primary tabs Amendment VII In suits at common law, where the value in controversy shall exceed twenty dollars, the right of trial by jury shall be preserved, and no fact tried by a jury, shall be otherwise reexamined in any court of the United States, than according to the rules of the common law. Wex Resources Criminal Law Criminal Procedure Jury Other Resources US Code: Title 18 Federal Rules of Criminal Procedure ‹ Sixth Amendment up Eighth Amendment › U.S. Constitution Toolbox Explanation of the Constitution - from the Congressional Research Service
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the Seventh Amendment as described in Scraped Law Snippet 2. The original contract allowed for jury trials, consistent with the Seventh Amendment's guarantee of this right in civil cases where the value in controversy exceeds twenty dollars. The modified text, however, eliminates the right to a court trial altogether, replacing it with mandatory out-of-court settlement via a coin flip. This clearly violates the preserved right to a jury trial.
